time.c(3) Library Functions Manual time.c(3)

NAME

time.c - ⏱️ High-precision timing utilities implementation

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define SOKOL_TIME_IMPL

Functions


uint64_t time_get_ns (void)
uint64_t time_get_realtime_ns (void)
void time_sleep_ns (uint64_t ns)
uint64_t time_elapsed_ns (uint64_t start_ns, uint64_t end_ns)
uint64_t time_timespec_to_ns (const struct timespec *ts)
void time_ns_to_timespec (uint64_t ns, struct timespec *ts)
bool timer_system_init (void)
void timer_system_destroy (void)
bool timer_start (const char *name)
double timer_stop (const char *name)
bool timer_is_initialized (void)
int format_uptime_hms (int hours, int minutes, int seconds, char *buffer, size_t buffer_size)
int time_pretty (uint64_t nanoseconds, int decimals, char *buffer, size_t buffer_size)
int time_pretty_now (int decimals, char *buffer, size_t buffer_size)
int time_human_readable_unsigned (uint64_t nanoseconds, char *buffer, size_t buffer_size)
int time_human_readable (int64_t nanoseconds, char *buffer, size_t buffer_size)
int time_human_readable_now (char *buffer, size_t buffer_size)
void adaptive_sleep_init (adaptive_sleep_state_t *state, const adaptive_sleep_config_t *config)
uint64_t adaptive_sleep_calculate (adaptive_sleep_state_t *state, size_t queue_depth, size_t target_depth)
void adaptive_sleep_do (adaptive_sleep_state_t *state, size_t queue_depth, size_t target_depth)
bool time_format_is_valid_strftime (const char *format_str)
int time_format_now (const char *format_str, char *buf, size_t buf_size)
asciichat_error_t time_format_safe (const char *format_str, char *buf, size_t buf_size)

Detailed Description

⏱️ High-precision timing utilities implementation

Definition in file util/time.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define SOKOL_TIME_IMPL

Definition at line 11 of file util/time.c.

Function Documentation

uint64_t adaptive_sleep_calculate (adaptive_sleep_state_t * state, size_t queue_depth, size_t target_depth)

Definition at line 622 of file util/time.c.

622                                                                                                           {
623   if (!state) {
624     return 0;
625   }
626 
627   const adaptive_sleep_config_t *cfg = &state->config;
628 
629   // Determine if we need to speed up or slow down
630   double desired_multiplier;
631 
632   if (queue_depth > target_depth) {
633     // Queue is building up - speed up processing
634     // The more items in queue, the faster we should process
635     size_t backlog = queue_depth - target_depth;
636 
637     // Calculate desired speed based on backlog
638     // Each item above target increases speed proportionally
639     // This ensures we drain the queue faster the more backed up we are
640     double backlog_factor = 1.0 + ((double)backlog / (double)(target_depth + 1));
641     desired_multiplier = cfg->min_speed_multiplier * backlog_factor;
642 
643     // Clamp to max speed
644     if (desired_multiplier > cfg->max_speed_multiplier) {
645       desired_multiplier = cfg->max_speed_multiplier;
646     }
647 
648     // Ramp up gradually based on speedup_rate
649     double delta = desired_multiplier - state->current_speed_multiplier;
650     state->current_speed_multiplier += delta * cfg->speedup_rate;
651 
652   } else {
653     // Queue is at or below target - slow down to baseline
654     desired_multiplier = cfg->min_speed_multiplier;
655 
656     // Ramp down gradually based on slowdown_rate
657     double delta = desired_multiplier - state->current_speed_multiplier;
658     state->current_speed_multiplier += delta * cfg->slowdown_rate;
659   }
660 
661   // Ensure we stay within configured bounds
662   if (state->current_speed_multiplier < cfg->min_speed_multiplier) {
663     state->current_speed_multiplier = cfg->min_speed_multiplier;
664   }
665   if (state->current_speed_multiplier > cfg->max_speed_multiplier) {
666     state->current_speed_multiplier = cfg->max_speed_multiplier;
667   }
668 
669   // Calculate actual sleep time: baseline / speed_multiplier
670   // Higher multiplier = shorter sleep = faster processing
671   uint64_t sleep_ns = (uint64_t)(cfg->baseline_sleep_ns / state->current_speed_multiplier);
672 
673   // Store for debugging
674   state->last_sleep_ns = sleep_ns;
675 
676   return sleep_ns;
677 }




Referenced by adaptive_sleep_do().

  adaptive_sleep_do (adaptive_sleep_state_t * state, size_t queue_depth, size_t

679                                                                                                {
680   uint64_t sleep_ns = adaptive_sleep_calculate(state, queue_depth, target_depth);
681 
682   if (sleep_ns > 0) {
683     platform_sleep_ns(sleep_ns);
684   }
685 }

void
  adaptive_sleep_init (adaptive_sleep_state_t * state, const
  adaptive_sleep_config_t * config)


Definition at line 607 of file util/time.c.


607                                                                                                {
608   if (!state || !config) {
609     return;
610   }
611 
612   // Copy configuration
613   state->config = *config;
614 
615   // Start at baseline speed (no speedup initially)
616   state->current_speed_multiplier = config->min_speed_multiplier;
617 
618   // Initial sleep is the baseline
619   state->last_sleep_ns = config->baseline_sleep_ns;
620 }

int
  format_uptime_hms (int hours, int minutes, int seconds, char * buffer, size_t
  buffer_size)


Definition at line 348 of file util/time.c.


348                                                                                              {
349   if (!buffer || buffer_size == 0) {
350     return -1;
351   }
352 
353   // Validate components
354   if (hours < 0 || minutes < 0 || minutes >= 60 || seconds < 0 || seconds >= 60) {
355     return -1;
356   }
357 
358   // Format as HH:MM:SS with zero-padding
359   int written = safe_snprintf(buffer, buffer_size, "%02d:%02d:%02d", hours, minutes, seconds);
360 
361   if (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= buffer_size) {
362     return -1;
363   }
364 
365   return written;
366 }






References buffer_size, and safe_snprintf().






uint64_t
  time_elapsed_ns (uint64_t start_ns, uint64_t end_ns)


Definition at line 150 of file util/time.c.


150                                                              {
151   // Handle wraparound (defensive - uint64_t won't wrap in practice at nanosecond resolution)
152   // but this is safe and handles any theoretical edge cases
153   if (end_ns >= start_ns) {
154     return end_ns - start_ns;
155   }
156   // Wraparound case (extremely unlikely)
157   return (UINT64_MAX - start_ns) + end_ns + 1;
158 }

    asciichat_instr_log_line(), audio_analysis_print_report(),
    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(),
    client_send_thread_func(), fps_frame_ns(),
    media_source_read_video(), media_source_seek(),
    mixer_process_excluding_source(),
    session_capture_get_current_fps(),
    session_capture_read_frame(), and
  session_client_like_run().






bool time_format_is_valid_strftime (const char *
  format_str)


Definition at line 697 of file util/time.c.


697                                                            {
698   if (!format_str) {
699     return false;
700   }
701 
702   for (const char *p = format_str; *p; p++) {
703     if (*p == '%') {
704       p++;
705       if (!*p) {
706         /* Unterminated % at end of string */
707         log_error("Invalid time format: unterminated %% at end");
708         return false;
709       }
710 
711       if (*p == '%') {
712         /* Escaped %% - this is valid, just skip it */
713         continue;
714       }
715 
716       /* Optional: flag characters (-, 0, +, space) */
717       if (*p == '-' || *p == '0' || *p == '+' || *p == ' ') {
718         p++;
719         if (!*p) {
720           log_error("Invalid time format: flag character without specifier");
721           return false;
722         }
723       }
724 
725       /* Optional: width specifier (digits or *) */
726       if (*p == '*' || isdigit((unsigned char)*p)) {
727         while (*p && (isdigit((unsigned char)*p) || *p == '*')) {
728           p++;
729         }
730         if (!*p) {
731           log_error("Invalid time format: width specifier without specifier character");
732           return false;
733         }
734       }
735 
736       /* Optional: precision specifier (.digits or .*) */
737       if (*p == '.') {
738         p++;
739         if (!*p) {
740           log_error("Invalid time format: precision specifier incomplete");
741           return false;
742         }
743         while (*p && (isdigit((unsigned char)*p) || *p == '*')) {
744           p++;
745         }
746         if (!*p) {
747           log_error("Invalid time format: precision specifier without specifier character");
748           return false;
749         }
750       }
751 
752       /* Optional: modifier characters (E for %Ex, O for %Ox) */
753       if (*p == 'E' || *p == 'O') {
754         p++;
755         if (!*p) {
756           log_error("Invalid time format: modifier character without specifier");
757           return false;
758         }
759       }
760 
761       /* Validate that the final character is a supported specifier */
762       if (!strchr(SUPPORTED_SPECIFIERS, *p)) {
763         log_error("Invalid time format: unsupported specifier %%%c (at position %ld)", *p, (p - format_str));
764         return false;
765       }
766     }
767   }
768 
769   return true;
770 }






Referenced by time_format_safe().






int
  time_format_now (const char * format_str, char * buf, size_t
  buf_size)


Definition at line 772 of file util/time.c.


772                                                                         {
773   if (!format_str || !buf || buf_size < 2) {
774     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "time_format_now: invalid arguments - format_str=%p, buf=%p, buf_size=%zu",
775               format_str, buf, buf_size);
776     return 0;
777   }
778 
779   /* Get current wall-clock time in nanoseconds */
780   uint64_t ts_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
781 
782   /* Extract seconds and nanoseconds */
783   time_t seconds = (time_t)(ts_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT);
784 
785   /* Convert seconds to struct tm */
786   struct tm tm_info;
787   platform_localtime(&seconds, &tm_info);
788 
789   /* Format using strftime */
790   size_t len = strftime(buf, buf_size, format_str, &tm_info);
791 
792   /* Check for strftime errors or buffer overflow */
793   if (len == 0) {
794     /* strftime returned 0 - either error or exact buffer fill */
795     log_debug("strftime returned 0 for format: %s", format_str);
796     return 0;
797   }
798 
799   if (len >= buf_size - 1) {
800     /* Buffer would overflow - strftime filled entire buffer or hit exact size */
801     log_error("time_format_now: buffer too small (need %zu, have %zu)", len + 1, buf_size);
802     return 0;
803   }
804 
805   return (int)len;
806 }






References platform_localtime(), and
    time_get_realtime_ns().

    time_format_safe().






asciichat_error_t
  time_format_safe (const char * format_str, char * buf, size_t
  buf_size)


Definition at line 808 of file util/time.c.


808                                                                                        {
809   /* Check NULL pointers */
810   if (!format_str) {
811     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "time_format_safe: format_str is NULL");
812   }
813 
814   if (!buf) {
815     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "time_format_safe: buf is NULL");
816   }
817 
818   /* Check minimum buffer size */
819   if (buf_size < 64) {
820     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "time_format_safe: buffer too small (minimum 64 bytes, got %zu)", buf_size);
821   }
822 
823   /* Validate format string */
824   if (!time_format_is_valid_strftime(format_str)) {
825     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "time_format_safe: invalid time format: %s", format_str);
826   }
827 
828   /* Format the time */
829   int result = time_format_now(format_str, buf, buf_size);
830   if (result <= 0) {
831     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "time_format_safe: strftime formatting failed");
832   }
833 
834   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
835 }






References time_format_is_valid_strftime(), and
    time_format_now().






uint64_t
  time_get_ns (void )


Definition at line 108 of file util/time.c.


108                            {
109   // sokol_time provides monotonic clock that never goes backwards
110   // stm_ns() converts ticks to nanoseconds
111   // Ensure sokol_time is initialized before use (defensive programming)
112   if (!g_sokol_time_initialized) {
113     stm_setup();
114     g_sokol_time_initialized = true;
115   }
116   return (uint64_t)stm_ns(stm_now());
117 }

uint64_t
  time_get_realtime_ns (void )


Definition at line 119 of file util/time.c.


119                                     {
120 #ifdef _WIN32
121   // Windows: Use GetSystemTimePreciseAsFileTime (Win8+) for high-precision wall clock
122   // FILETIME is 100-nanosecond intervals since January 1, 1601
123   FILETIME ft;
124   GetSystemTimePreciseAsFileTime(&ft);
125   // Convert FILETIME to nanoseconds
126   uint64_t filetime = ((uint64_t)ft.dwHighDateTime << 32) | ft.dwLowDateTime;
127   // Convert 100-nanosecond intervals to nanoseconds
128   return filetime * 100;
129 #else
130   // Get wall-clock (real-time) timestamp
131   struct timespec ts;
132   if (clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts) != 0) {
133     // Fallback on error (unlikely)
134     return 0;
135   }
136   return time_timespec_to_ns(&ts);
137 #endif
138 }






References time_timespec_to_ns().

    asciichat_error_stats_record(), asciichat_instr_log_line(),
    asciichat_set_errno(), connection_attempt_tcp(),
    connection_attempt_websocket(), connection_check_timeout(),
    connection_context_init(), consensus_metrics_measure(),
    consensus_state_compute_election(),
    database_session_is_migration_ready(),
    database_session_start_migration(),
    get_current_time_formatted(),
    handle_image_frame_h265_packet(), log_json_async_safe(),
    log_msg(), log_plain_msg(), log_terminal_msg(), and
    time_format_now().






int
  time_human_readable (int64_t nanoseconds, char * buffer, size_t
  buffer_size)


Definition at line 570 of file util/time.c.


570                                                                                {
571   if (!buffer || buffer_size == 0) {
572     return -1;
573   }
574 
575   // Determine direction (past vs future)
576   bool is_future = nanoseconds < 0;
577   uint64_t abs_ns = is_future ? (uint64_t)(-nanoseconds) : (uint64_t)nanoseconds;
578 
579   // Convert nanoseconds to seconds for threshold checking
580   double total_seconds = (double)abs_ns / NS_PER_SEC;
581   int minutes = (int)(total_seconds / 60);
582   int hours = minutes / 60;
583   int days = hours / 24;
584   int months = days / 30; // Approximate
585   int years = months / 12;
586 
587   const char *suffix = is_future ? "from now" : "ago";
588   int written =
589       format_human_readable_time_ago(total_seconds, minutes, hours, days, months, years, suffix, buffer, buffer_size);
590 
591   if (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= buffer_size) {
592     return -1;
593   }
594 
595   return written;
596 }

void
  time_ns_to_timespec (uint64_t ns, struct timespec * ts)

172                                                            {
173   if (!ts) {
174     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "null ts");

int
  time_pretty (uint64_t nanoseconds, int decimals, char * buffer, size_t
  buffer_size)


Definition at line 418 of file util/time.c.


418                                                                                       {
419   if (!buffer || buffer_size == 0) {
420     return -1;
421   }
422 
423   // Handle zero
424   if (nanoseconds == 0) {
425     int written = safe_snprintf(buffer, buffer_size, "0 ns");
426     return (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= buffer_size) ? -1 : written;
427   }
428 
429   // Determine decimals for this range if not explicitly set
430   int actual_decimals = decimals;
431   if (decimals == -1) {
432     if (nanoseconds < NS_PER_MS_INT) {
433       actual_decimals = 3; // ns/µs
434     } else if (nanoseconds < NS_PER_SEC_INT) {
435       actual_decimals = 3; // ms
436     } else if (nanoseconds < NS_PER_MIN_INT) {
437       actual_decimals = 2; // seconds
438     } else {
439       actual_decimals = 3; // colon notation
440     }
441   }
442 
443   // Clamp decimals to valid range
444   if (actual_decimals < 0)
445     actual_decimals = 0;
446   if (actual_decimals > 9)
447     actual_decimals = 9;
448 
449   int written = 0;
450 
451   // Nanoseconds (< 1µs)
452   if (nanoseconds < NS_PER_US_INT) {
453     written = safe_snprintf(buffer, buffer_size, "%llu ns", (unsigned long long)nanoseconds);
454   }
455   // Microseconds (< 1ms)
456   else if (nanoseconds < NS_PER_MS_INT) {
457     double us = (double)nanoseconds / NS_PER_US;
458     char temp_buf[64];
459     // Use a direct snprintf with large enough buffer first
460     snprintf(temp_buf, sizeof(temp_buf), "%.*f µs", actual_decimals, us);
461     strip_trailing_zeros(temp_buf);
462     written = (int)strlen(temp_buf);
463     if (written > 0 && written < (int)buffer_size) {
464       SAFE_STRNCPY(buffer, temp_buf, buffer_size);
465     } else {
466       written = -1;
467     }
468   }
469   // Milliseconds (< 1s)
470   else if (nanoseconds < NS_PER_SEC_INT) {
471     double ms = (double)nanoseconds / NS_PER_MS;
472     char temp_buf[64];
473     snprintf(temp_buf, sizeof(temp_buf), "%.*f ms", actual_decimals, ms);
474     strip_trailing_zeros(temp_buf);
475     written = (int)strlen(temp_buf);
476     if (written > 0 && written < (int)buffer_size) {
477       SAFE_STRNCPY(buffer, temp_buf, buffer_size);
478     } else {
479       written = -1;
480     }
481   }
482   // Seconds (< 1 minute)
483   else if (nanoseconds < NS_PER_MIN_INT) {
484     double s = (double)nanoseconds / NS_PER_SEC;
485     char temp_buf[64];
486     snprintf(temp_buf, sizeof(temp_buf), "%.*f s", actual_decimals, s);
487     strip_trailing_zeros(temp_buf);
488     written = (int)strlen(temp_buf);
489     if (written > 0 && written < (int)buffer_size) {
490       SAFE_STRNCPY(buffer, temp_buf, buffer_size);
491     } else {
492       written = -1;
493     }
494   }
495   // Minutes (< 1 hour) - use colon notation: M:SS.frac
496   else if (nanoseconds < NS_PER_HOUR_INT) {
497     uint64_t total_seconds = nanoseconds / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
498     uint64_t minutes = total_seconds / 60;
499     uint64_t seconds = total_seconds % 60;
500     uint64_t remainder_ns = nanoseconds % NS_PER_SEC_INT;
501     double fraction = (double)remainder_ns / NS_PER_SEC;
502 
503     char temp_buf[64];
504     if (actual_decimals == 0) {
505       snprintf(temp_buf, sizeof(temp_buf), "%llu:%02llu", (unsigned long long)minutes, (unsigned long long)seconds);
506     } else {
507       snprintf(temp_buf, sizeof(temp_buf), "%llu:%02llu.%.*f", (unsigned long long)minutes, (unsigned long long)seconds,
508                actual_decimals, fraction);
509       strip_trailing_zeros(temp_buf);
510     }
511 
512     written = (int)strlen(temp_buf);
513     if (written > 0 && written < (int)buffer_size) {
514       SAFE_STRNCPY(buffer, temp_buf, buffer_size);
515     } else {
516       written = -1;
517     }
518   }
519   // Hours+ - use colon notation: H:MM:SS or HH:MM:SS
520   else {
521     uint64_t total_seconds = nanoseconds / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
522     uint64_t hours = total_seconds / 3600;
523     uint64_t remaining = total_seconds % 3600;
524     uint64_t minutes = remaining / 60;
525     uint64_t seconds = remaining % 60;
526 
527     written = safe_snprintf(buffer, buffer_size, "%llu:%02llu:%02llu", (unsigned long long)hours,
528                             (unsigned long long)minutes, (unsigned long long)seconds);
529   }
530 
531   if (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= buffer_size) {
532     return -1;
533   }
534 
535   return written;
536 }






References buffer_size, and safe_snprintf().

void
  time_sleep_ns (uint64_t ns)


Definition at line 140 of file util/time.c.


140                                 {
141   // Use platform abstraction for cross-platform sleep
142   // Convert nanoseconds to microseconds (1 us = 1000 ns)
143   unsigned int usec = (unsigned int)(ns / 1000);
144   if (usec == 0 && ns > 0) {
145     usec = 1; // Minimum 1 microsecond
146   }
147   platform_sleep_us(usec);
148 }






References platform_sleep_us().

  time_timespec_to_ns (const struct timespec * ts)


Definition at line 164 of file util/time.c.

165   if (!ts) {
166     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "null ts");
167     return 0;
168   }
169   return (uint64_t)ts->tv_sec * NS_PER_SEC_INT + (uint64_t)ts->tv_nsec;

bool timer_start (const char * name)


Definition at line 234 of file util/time.c.


234                                    {
235   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_timer_manager.lifecycle)) {
236     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Timer system not initialized");
237     return false;
238   }
239 
240   if (!name) {
241     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Timer name is NULL");
242     return false;
243   }
244 
245   rwlock_wrlock(&g_timer_manager.rwlock);
246 
247   // Check if timer already exists - this is an error, timers must be unique
248   timer_record_t *existing = NULL;
249   HASH_FIND_STR(g_timer_manager.timers, name, existing);
250 
251   if (existing) {
252     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_timer_manager.rwlock);
253     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Timer '%s' already exists - timers must have unique names", name);
254     return false;
255   }
256 
257   // Create new timer record
258   timer_record_t *timer = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(timer_record_t), timer_record_t *);
259   if (!timer) {
260     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_timer_manager.rwlock);
261     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate timer record");
262     return false;
263   }
264 
265   // Use platform-safe string duplication
266   size_t name_len = strlen(name) + 1;
267   timer->name = SAFE_MALLOC(name_len, char *);
268   if (!timer->name) {
269     SAFE_FREE(timer);
270     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_timer_manager.rwlock);
271     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate timer name");
272     return false;
273   }
274   SAFE_STRNCPY(timer->name, name, name_len);
275 
276   timer->start_ticks = stm_now();
277 
278   // Add to hashtable
279   HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, g_timer_manager.timers, timer->name, strlen(timer->name), timer);
280 
281   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_timer_manager.rwlock);
282   return true;
283 }






References lifecycle_is_initialized().






double
  timer_stop (const char * name)


Definition at line 285 of file util/time.c.


285                                     {
286   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_timer_manager.lifecycle)) {
287     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Timer system not initialized");
288     return -1.0;
289   }
290 
291   if (!name) {
292     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Timer name is NULL");
293     return -1.0;
294   }
295 
296   rwlock_wrlock(&g_timer_manager.rwlock);
297 
298   // Find timer
299   timer_record_t *timer = NULL;
300   HASH_FIND_STR(g_timer_manager.timers, name, timer);
301 
302   if (!timer) {
303     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_timer_manager.rwlock);
304     log_dev("Timer '%s' not found (may have already been stopped)", name);
305     return -1.0;
306   }
307 
308   // Calculate elapsed time in nanoseconds for maximum precision
309   uint64_t end_ticks = stm_now();
310   uint64_t elapsed_ticks = stm_diff(end_ticks, timer->start_ticks);
311   double elapsed_ns = stm_ns(elapsed_ticks);
312 
313   // Format duration for human-readable logging
314   char duration_str[32];
315   time_pretty((uint64_t)elapsed_ns, -1, duration_str, sizeof(duration_str));
316 
317   // Log the result (dev level - only shown with --verbose)
318   log_dev("Timer '%s': %s", name, duration_str);
319 
320   // Save pointers to free BEFORE removing from hashtable
321   char *timer_name = timer->name;
322 
323   // Remove from hashtable (HASH_DEL only removes from hash, doesn't free memory)
324   HASH_DEL(g_timer_manager.timers, timer);
325 
326   // Free timer->name first (this was allocated separately)
327   SAFE_FREE(timer_name);
328 
329   // Nullify the timer->name field so debug_free doesn't try to access it
330   // when freeing the timer structure itself
331   timer->name = NULL;
332 
333   // Free the timer record itself
334   SAFE_FREE(timer);
335 
336   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_timer_manager.rwlock);
337   return elapsed_ns; // Return nanoseconds
338 }






References lifecycle_is_initialized(), and
    time_pretty().






void
  timer_system_destroy (void )


Definition at line 207 of file util/time.c.


207                                 {
208   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_timer_manager.lifecycle)) {
209     return;
210   }
211 
212   rwlock_wrlock(&g_timer_manager.rwlock);
213 
214   // Free all timer records
215   int timer_count = 0;
216   timer_record_t *current, *tmp;
217   HASH_ITER(hh, g_timer_manager.timers, current, tmp) {
218     HASH_DEL(g_timer_manager.timers, current);
219     SAFE_FREE(current->name);
220     SAFE_FREE(current);
221     timer_count++;
222   }
223 
224   g_timer_manager.timers = NULL;
225 
226   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_timer_manager.rwlock);
227 
228   /* Shutdown lifecycle and destroy rwlock together */
229   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_timer_manager.lifecycle);
230 
231   log_debug("Timer system cleaned up (freed %d timers)", timer_count);
232 }






References lifecycle_is_initialized(), and
    lifecycle_shutdown().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().






bool timer_system_init (void )


Definition at line 185 of file util/time.c.


185                              {
186   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_timer_manager.lifecycle)) {
187     return true;
188   }
189 
190   // Initialize sokol_time
191   stm_setup();
192 
193   // Initialize rwlock and lifecycle together
194   g_timer_manager.lifecycle.sync_type = LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK;
195   g_timer_manager.lifecycle.sync.rwlock = &g_timer_manager.rwlock;
196   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_timer_manager.lifecycle, "timer_manager")) {
197     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Failed to initialize timer system (already initialized by another thread)");
198     return true; // Another thread already initialized it
199   }
200 
201   g_timer_manager.timers = NULL;
202 
203   log_dev("Timer system initialized");
204   return true;
205 }






References lifecycle_init(), and
    lifecycle_is_initialized().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_init().








Variable
  Documentation




lifecycle_t
  lifecycle


Lifecycle state machine for init/shutdown synchronization.


Definition at line 35 of file util/time.c.






rwlock_t
  rwlock


Read-write lock for thread-safe access (uthash requires external
    locking).


Definition at line 34 of file util/time.c.

  timers
